full_prompt: Are there broad principles explaining when local mathematical constraints force a global structure? The starting idea is that enough small-scale consistency should determine the whole object, but the amount and type of local information needed is uncertain.
chosen:
I start by looking for recent mathematics/TCS work where “local consistency implies global object” is made quantitative, especially agreement theorems, high-dimensional expansion, and cohomological obstructions.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"2018 2025 local consistency global structure agreement theorem high dimensional expanders cohomology local to global mathematics","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: The search surfaces a cluster around high-dimensional expanders and agreement testing: Dinur–Kaufman-style agreement theorems, cosystolic expansion, and locally testable codes. It also finds sheaf/cohomology language for local-to-global obstructions, plus Helly-type and CSP/property-testing formulations. Representative OpenAlex works returned include `https://openalex.org/W2963349478`, `https://openalex.org/W3046284304`, `https://openalex.org/W4289274471`, `https://openalex.org/W4312455649`, and `https://openalex.org/W4387200957`.

The rough hypothesis is that “enough overlap consistency” should force a global structure, but I need an initial formal genome before testing where it is vague.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Small local pieces determine a global object when their pairwise or bounded-overlap restrictions agree on a sufficiently connected pattern of overlaps; expansion propagates agreement from local patches to the whole domain.","niche_genome":"Finite mathematical structures from 2018–2025 work on high-dimensional expanders, property testing, locally testable codes, CSPs, and combinatorial topology.","observation_genome":"Agreement theorems and local testability results suggest that checking consistency on small faces, neighborhoods, or constraints can certify proximity to a single global assignment.","limitation_genome":"The proposal does not yet specify what kind of connectivity is enough, how topological obstructions enter, or how exact versus approximate local consistency changes the conclusion.","delta_genome":"Reframe several local-to-global theorems as instances of one principle: overlap connectivity plus redundancy turns local consistency into global rigidity.","claim_genome":"A broad principle likely exists: sufficiently redundant small-scale consistency over an expanding overlap geometry forces a unique or nearly unique global structure."}}
```

[result]: Proposed v1 is coherent but too close to a slogan. It identifies expansion and overlap redundancy, but lacks a precise obstruction theory: cycles, cohomology, holonomy, and stability constants are not yet integrated. The next step should compare this to a known high-dimensional-expander agreement result.

I compare v1 against a parent paper from the search cluster, treating high-dimensional agreement testing as the closest known genome.

```action
{"tool":"genome_diff","parent_id":"https://openalex.org/W2963349478","proposed_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Small local pieces determine a global object when their pairwise or bounded-overlap restrictions agree on a sufficiently connected pattern of overlaps; expansion propagates agreement from local patches to the whole domain.","niche_genome":"Finite mathematical structures from 2018–2025 work on high-dimensional expanders, property testing, locally testable codes, CSPs, and combinatorial topology.","observation_genome":"Agreement theorems and local testability results suggest that checking consistency on small faces, neighborhoods, or constraints can certify proximity to a single global assignment.","limitation_genome":"The proposal does not yet specify what kind of connectivity is enough, how topological obstructions enter, or how exact versus approximate local consistency changes the conclusion.","delta_genome":"Reframe several local-to-global theorems as instances of one principle: overlap connectivity plus redundancy turns local consistency into global rigidity.","claim_genome":"A broad principle likely exists: sufficiently redundant small-scale consistency over an expanding overlap geometry forces a unique or nearly unique global structure."}}
```

[result]: The diff says v1 matches the parent’s agreement-expansion mechanism but is less quantitative. Missing genes include: the geometry must often be a simplicial or incidence complex, local functions live on faces or neighborhoods, and the theorem usually proves “near agreement implies near global function,” not exact uniqueness. The diff also flags that topological obstruction should be represented by low-dimensional cohomology or cocycles, not merely by “connectivity.”

I check whether the proposed mechanism is already a named broad principle or whether a refined synthesis could still be novel.

```action
{"tool":"novelty_check","mechanism":"Local mathematical constraints force global structure when imposed on an expanding overlap geometry with enough redundancy; approximate local agreement propagates to proximity to a global object, modulo topological or cohomological obstructions.","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025}
```

[result]: Novelty is moderate. Agreement expansion, cosystolic expansion, sheaf cohomology, and local testability each contain pieces of the mechanism, but the unified “expansion plus obstruction-vanishing plus stability” formulation is not usually stated as a cross-domain principle. The check recommends adding failure modes: nontrivial cycles/holonomy, poor overlap expansion, and instability of the constraint theory.

The missing piece is a more explicit obstruction language, so I search for recent work connecting local-to-global consistency with sheaves, cocycles, or cohomology.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"2018 2025 sheaf cohomology local to global consistency constraints high dimensional expander cosystolic expansion agreement theorem","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```

[result]: Results emphasize that local data often form sections of a sheaf or cochain complex, and global consistency is equivalent to triviality of a cocycle or vanishing of an obstruction class. High-dimensional-expander papers provide quantitative versions: cosystolic or coboundary expansion gives robust correction from locally consistent data to global sections. Relevant OpenAlex IDs returned include `https://openalex.org/W3046284304`, `https://openalex.org/W4289274471`, `https://openalex.org/W4312455649`, `https://openalex.org/W4387200957`, and `https://openalex.org/W4401338561`.

I read the most relevant obstruction-oriented item to refine the hypothesis from “connected overlaps” to “expanding complex plus vanishing/stable cohomology.”

```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"https://openalex.org/W4289274471"}
```

[result]: The paper frames local data as assignments on cells/faces with compatibility maps, making global solutions into global sections or cocycles satisfying boundary conditions. Its key lesson is that local agreement can fail globally because of holonomy around cycles: every small overlap may look consistent while a nontrivial cohomology class prevents a single global object. Quantitative expansion strengthens this by making small local inconsistency correctable, provided the relevant cohomological obstructions vanish or are bounded away by cosystolic expansion.

With expansion, sheaf/cohomology obstruction, and approximate stability now identified, I revise the proposal into a sharper local-to-global principle.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Local constraints force global structure when the local data form a sheaf-like or cochain-like system over an overlap complex satisfying three conditions: dense coverage of the object by bounded-size patches, quantitative expansion of the incidence/overlap complex so that agreement propagates, and vanishing or robust isolation of the relevant low-dimensional cohomological obstructions. In the approximate case, coboundary/cosystolic or agreement expansion converts small local inconsistency into bounded distance from a genuine global section.","niche_genome":"Finite and combinatorial mathematics in the 2018–2025 window: high-dimensional expanders, agreement testing, locally testable codes, CSP/property testing, simplicial complexes, incidence geometries, and sheaf-theoretic formulations of consistency. The principle is aimed at discrete structures where local restrictions, overlaps, and correction distance can be made quantitative.","observation_genome":"Recent agreement-expansion and local-testability results show that assignments on small faces or neighborhoods that agree on overlaps are often close to a single global assignment. Conversely, examples with nontrivial cycles, weak overlap expansion, or holonomy show that pairwise/local consistency alone is insufficient: the missing datum is not more local checks of the same kind, but an obstruction-vanishing and stability condition.","limitation_genome":"This is not yet a universal theorem for all mathematics: smooth PDE integrability, algebraic geometry gluing, model theory, and infinite structures may require different compactness or regularity hypotheses. Even in finite settings, the constants and required dimension of local checks depend on the category of objects, the norm for approximation, and the relevant cohomology theory.","delta_genome":"The refinement over the rough idea is to replace 'enough small-scale consistency' by a three-part criterion: overlap expansion propagates information, cohomological triviality removes global holonomy obstructions, and quantitative stability turns approximate local satisfaction into proximity to a global object. This synthesizes high-dimensional-expander agreement theorems with sheaf/cocycle obstruction language.","claim_genome":"Broad principle: local mathematical constraints determine global structure exactly or approximately iff the constraint system is placed on an expanding overlap geometry and its only potential global obstructions vanish or are quantitatively stable. The amount of local information needed is therefore governed less by patch size alone than by three invariants: expansion of the overlap complex, the degree of the obstruction cohomology, and the stability modulus converting local error into global correction."}}
```

[result]: Final refined proposal accepted as a cross-domain hypothesis rather than a single theorem. It identifies when local consistency should force global structure, why it can fail, and what quantitative parameters determine the required local information.

full_prompt: Propose a cognitive intervention for reducing smartphone distraction using implementation intentions and attentional control training. These approaches can reduce impulsive checking, but effects often fade because cues are not adapted to individual habits. Target a cheaper, personalized intervention that predicts and blocks high-risk checking episodes with minimal user burden.
chosen:
Rationale: A low-burden intervention should combine implementation intentions (“If I am in cue/context X, then I will do Y instead”) with brief attentional-control practice, but personalize trigger timing using passive phone-context data so prompts/blocks occur only during predicted high-risk moments.

```action search
{"query":"2025 smartphone distraction implementation intentions attentional control personalized just-in-time intervention high-risk checking episodes evidence"}
```

[Result] Recent digital self-control and JITAI research supports context-aware, just-in-time interventions for smartphone overuse, with passive sensing and individualized risk prediction improving relevance and reducing burden. Implementation intentions remain evidence-based for habit disruption, while attentional-control training shows promise but benefits are stronger when practiced in cue-relevant contexts.

Rationale: The confirmed direction is a personalized just-in-time cognitive intervention: learn each user’s high-risk checking contexts, pre-commit if–then alternatives, and deploy brief attentional-control blocks only when risk is high.

```action
propose: ACT-R-Informed POMDP Framework for Human-AI Teamwork in Air Traffic Control — Design a human-AI teaming model that reduces aircraft conflict-resolution time without increasing controller cognitive workload. The framework uses a two-layer architecture: an ACT-R-based human cognition layer estimates attention, memory retrieval, decision latency, and workload, while a POMDP-based AI assistance layer selects advisory actions under uncertainty about traffic state, controller intent, and cognitive state. The POMDP treats controller workload and trust as latent states, updated using observable cues such as response time, eye-gaze shifts, communication load, and accepted or rejected advisories. ACT-R simulations constrain the POMDP reward and transition models so that recommended actions remain cognitively plausible. The system optimizes joint team performance with a reward function penalizing conflict-resolution delay, unsafe separation loss, excessive advisory frequency, attention switching, memory load, and high estimated workload. Evaluation compares baseline controller-only, conventional automation, and ACT-R-informed POMDP assistance in simulated ATC scenarios using conflict-resolution time, separation safety, NASA-TLX or modeled workload, intervention acceptance rate, and controller response latency.
```

full_prompt:
You are a senior researcher in Generative Models / Computer Vision.
Based on your expertise, generate a new research idea for the following frontier question.

**Frontier Question**:
DreamWorld jointly models video pixels and world features but cannot interact with or reason about the generated world. What mechanism would enable an interactive world model — where actions taken in the generated world causally affect future world states in physically consistent ways?

Generate a new research idea for the frontier question.

Return ONLY valid JSON. Do not wrap it in Markdown fences.
{
  "idea": "<short name of the proposed idea, 5-15 words>",
  "idea_description": "<150-250 words describing the full research idea>",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "<specific problem, dataset, population, or phenomenon>",
    "core_method": "<technical method or mechanism, not a vague goal>",
    "limitation_addressed": "<frontier limitation repaired by this idea>",
    "expected_result": "<scientific result or claim if successful>"
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": ["<paper title or source idea used by this proposal>", "..."],
    "reused_parts": ["<specific method, evidence, data, or problem framing reused from the sources>", "..."],
    "changed_parts": ["<specific modified, replaced, combined, or newly added part>", "..."],
    "why_next_step": "<why this idea follows from the provided context>",
    "lineage_risk": "<most likely way this source connection could be wrong>"
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "<concrete experiment, benchmark, proof, or analysis setup>",
    "baselines": ["<baseline>", "..."],
    "metrics": ["<metric>", "..."],
    "risks": ["<risk>", "..."]
  }
}
chosen:
I’ll first map the space from generative world models to action-conditioned models, because the key missing mechanism is likely not better rendering but causal controllability. 
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"generative world model video action conditioned interactive environments causal dynamics pixels features","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: The search surfaces three clusters: latent dynamics models for control, action-conditioned video/world generation, and interactive generative environments. Highly relevant hits include “Learning Latent Dynamics for Planning from Pixels” (OpenAlex: W2911308517), “Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models” (OpenAlex: W4387700756), and “Genie: Generative Interactive Environments” (OpenAlex: W4390789553). These suggest that interaction requires explicitly modeling action-conditioned transitions, not only unconditional video synthesis.

DreamWorld-like systems seem adjacent to recent text/video/3D world generation, so I’ll narrow toward controllability and physical consistency in generated visual worlds.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"DreamWorld generated world features video pixels interactive world model physically consistent actions","year_min":2022,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: Results point to text-to-3D/world generation and video generative models that model geometry or scene features but often lack closed-loop action interfaces. Related papers include “DreamFusion: Text-to-3D using 2D Diffusion” (OpenAlex: W4312921688), “GAIA-1: A Generative World Model for Autonomous Driving” (OpenAlex: W4388663847), and “DreamWorld: Learning a Generalizable 3D Object-Scene Representation from Monocular Videos” (OpenAlex: W4388466742). The limitation is consistent: generated scenes are visually plausible but do not expose a verified causal transition operator.

Next I’ll search specifically for mechanisms that bind actions to causal state change, since that is the frontier question’s core.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"action conditioned diffusion video model causal physical consistency latent dynamics controllable world model","year_min":2020,"year_max":2025,"k":5}
```
[result]: The strongest relevant papers are “Genie: Generative Interactive Environments” (W4390789553), “GAIA-1: A Generative World Model for Autonomous Driving” (W4388663847), and diffusion/video policies that learn action-conditioned transitions from demonstrations. Genie is especially relevant because it learns latent actions from video and produces controllable future frames, but it does not explicitly enforce physical causal invariants. GAIA-1 is action/ego-conditioned but restricted to driving logs and does not generalize to arbitrary generated worlds.

I’ll read Genie first because it directly addresses generative environments with actions.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4390789553"}
```
[result]: Genie trains a foundation world model from internet videos by tokenizing video into latent visual tokens, learning latent action codes, and predicting future frames conditioned on previous frames and actions. Its key contribution is that interactive control can emerge without labeled actions by discovering a discrete latent action space. However, its causal validity is learned from pixel correlations; actions can control generation, but there is no explicit physical state, object persistence constraint, or intervention-tested transition graph.

I’ll read DreamerV3/PlaNet-style latent dynamics work to recover the control-theoretic side that video generators lack.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4387700756"}
```
[result]: DreamerV3 learns a recurrent state-space world model from pixels and trains policies by imagination inside the learned latent dynamics. The reusable mechanism is a compact latent transition model with reward/value learning and multi-step rollout consistency. Its limitation for DreamWorld-style generation is that it is task/environment-specific and optimized for RL benchmarks rather than open-ended generated scenes with high-fidelity rendering.

I’ll read GAIA-1 because it is a practical high-capacity action-conditioned world generator with physical driving constraints.
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_id":"W4388663847"}
```
[result]: GAIA-1 generates realistic driving video conditioned on text, image context, and ego-actions, learning plausible futures in an autonomous-driving domain. It demonstrates that action-conditioned generative video can preserve some physical consistency when data contains dense state-action trajectories. The weakness is that causal structure is implicit: it predicts likely video under logged controls, but does not expose object-centric counterfactual dynamics or guarantee that interventions produce physically valid state updates.

Given these readings, I’ll check novelty around explicitly combining object-centric causal transition graphs with diffusion/world generation.
```action
{"tool":"novelty_check","mechanism":"object-centric causal transition operator supervising action-conditioned diffusion world model with intervention-consistency losses and differentiable physics residuals","year_min":2018,"year_max":2025}
```

full_prompt: 研究生成式模型在网络钓鱼邮件检测中的对抗风险，评估攻击者生成变体文本对检测器的影响。请用自由文本提出实验设计。
rejected:
我不先做范围检索，直接提出一个大致实验设计：把生成式变体看作钓鱼邮件检测器可能遇到的一种干扰，并粗略比较不同检测方法在改写邮件上的表现。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"实验机制：准备一批钓鱼邮件和正常邮件，训练若干钓鱼邮件检测器，然后使用生成式模型对钓鱼邮件进行改写，观察检测器是否仍能识别这些邮件。改写可以包括换词、改变语气、调整格式、增加或减少细节等。基本想法是，如果检测器主要依赖表层文本特征，那么生成式改写可能会降低其检测效果。","niche_genome":"实验对象是钓鱼邮件检测任务，可使用公开邮件数据集或自行构造的小规模样本。检测器可以包括传统机器学习模型、深度学习模型，以及基于大语言模型提示的分类方法。生成式模型用于产生若干不同版本的钓鱼邮件变体，但具体模型、数据来源和攻击策略可以根据实验条件灵活选择。","observation_genome":"主要观察指标包括准确率、召回率、F1 值，以及改写后钓鱼邮件被误判为正常邮件的比例。可以比较原始钓鱼邮件和生成式改写邮件上的检测性能差异。如果条件允许，也可以人工检查部分生成邮件，确认其仍然具有钓鱼意图并且可读性较好。","limitation_genome":"该实验可能受到数据规模、生成模型质量、提示词设计和检测器选择的影响。生成的钓鱼邮件变体未必真实反映攻击者行为，也可能过于简单或过于夸张。不同模型之间的比较也可能不完全公平，因此结果更适合作为初步探索，而不是最终结论。","delta_genome":"相比只在静态邮件数据集上评估检测器，本实验加入了生成式改写后的测试样本，用来粗略衡量检测器面对文本变化时的鲁棒性。可以进一步扩展为比较不同改写方式、不同检测器类型，以及加入简单防御策略前后的性能变化。","claim_genome":"预期结论是，生成式改写可能会降低部分钓鱼邮件检测器的性能，尤其是依赖关键词或固定模板的模型。更复杂的模型可能表现更稳健，但仍可能受到语气、格式和表达方式变化的影响。因此，钓鱼邮件检测器的评估不应只关注原始测试集准确率，也应考虑生成式文本变体带来的鲁棒性问题。"}}
```

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"推荐出行时间为 9 月下旬至 10 月中旬，尤其是国庆后半段至 10 月中旬更适合兼顾安大略与魁北克枫叶。11 天路线建议为：上海—多伦多 2 晚—尼亚加拉瀑布 1 晚—渥太华 2 晚—蒙特利尔 2 晚—魁北克城 2 晚—返程。第 1 天从上海飞多伦多，入住市中心或 Union Station 附近；第 2 天游览皇家安大略博物馆、安大略美术馆、多伦多大学与海滨，傍晚可登 CN Tower 或在 Harbourfront 看秋日湖景。第 3 天乘 GO/VIA+巴士或一日团前往尼亚加拉瀑布，下午走瀑布步道、Hornblower 游船或 Journey Behind the Falls，夜宿瀑布区方便看夜景灯光。\n\n第 4 天上午可沿 Niagara Parkway 赏枫，若时间允许顺访 Niagara-on-the-Lake 小镇，下午返回多伦多并乘 VIA Rail 前往渥太华，夜宿市中心。第 5 天安排国会山、拜沃德市场、里多运河、加拿大国家美术馆；第 6 天过桥到加蒂诺参观加拿大历史博物馆，并可去 Gatineau Park 或 Pink Lake 一带短途赏枫，晚上乘 VIA Rail 到蒙特利尔或继续住渥太华一晚视车次而定。若偏舒适，建议第 6 晚住渥太华，第 7 天上午坐火车去蒙特利尔。\n\n第 7—8 天住蒙特利尔老城或市中心。安排圣母大教堂、老港、雅克卡蒂埃广场、蒙特利尔美术馆，以及皇家山公园俯瞰城市秋色；餐饮可尝试 smoked meat、bagel、poutine 和法式小馆。第 9 天乘 VIA Rail 或城际巴士前往魁北克城，入住老城内或 Saint-Jean 街附近，下午游览芳堤娜城堡、达弗林平台、小香普兰街和城墙。第 10 天深度游魁北克老城与文明博物馆，半日去蒙莫朗西瀑布或奥尔良岛赏枫；第 11 天从魁北克城经蒙特利尔/多伦多转机返回上海，若机票更便宜也可第 10 晚回蒙特利尔住宿后返程。\n\n预算按每人估算：上海往返加拿大东部含转机机票约 8000—12000 元；10 晚中档酒店双人分摊约 7000—9500 元；VIA Rail/巴士/当地交通约 1800—2800 元；餐饮每天 350—500 元，约 3800—5500 元；门票与体验约 2000—3000 元；保险、通信和机动约 1000—1500 元。合计约 23600—28300 元，若要稳定压在 2.8 万内，建议提前 2—4 个月锁定机票和 VIA Rail Escape/经济舱票，酒店选择火车站步行或地铁可达的舒适型品牌，尼亚加拉游船和 CN Tower 二选一即可。\n\n交通建议以公共交通为主：城市间优先 VIA Rail Corridor，座位舒适、行李方便，路线为 Toronto—Ottawa—Montreal—Quebec City；多伦多至尼亚加拉可用 GO Transit 季节班次、VIA Rail+公交或报名小团。住宿尽量选 Toronto Union Station、Ottawa Downtown/ByWard、Montreal Old Montreal 或 Downtown、Quebec Old City 周边，减少拖行李换乘。注意事项：加拿大秋季早晚温差大，需带防风外套、薄羽绒或抓绒；枫叶高峰每年波动，魁北克段可能更早变色；国庆及感恩节前后住宿涨价明显；博物馆部分日期闭馆，建议提前预约热门景点与火车票。"}
```

full_prompt: Propose an account of implicit bias change using evaluative conditioning and the Implicit Association Test. Current interventions often reduce test scores briefly but fail to produce durable behavior change. Target a longer-term metric linking association strength, explicit goals, and observed social decisions.
chosen:
Rationale: I’ll confirm one targeted premise: whether brief implicit-bias interventions are still understood as producing short-lived IAT changes with weak evidence for durable behavioral change.

```action search
{"query":"2025 implicit bias interventions IAT effects short-lived durable behavior change evidence"}
```

[result] Recent reviews and meta-analytic discussions continue to indicate that many implicit-bias interventions can shift IAT scores briefly, but durable change and reliable transfer to real-world behavior remain limited or inconsistent. This supports framing the proposal around longer-term coupling among associative strength, explicit goals, and observed decisions.

Rationale: Propose a cognitive-science account in which evaluative conditioning changes implicit associations only when repeatedly aligned with explicit egalitarian goals and reinforced through consequential social choices; the key metric tracks whether association change predicts behavior over time under goal-relevant contexts.

```action propose
{
  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "name": "Goal-Coupled Evaluative Conditioning Account of Durable Implicit Bias Change",
    "core_claim": "Evaluative conditioning can reduce implicit bias durably only when new positive outgroup associations are repeatedly paired with active explicit goals and then expressed in observable social decisions. IAT change alone is treated as a proximal association-strength measure, not as evidence of durable behavioral change.",
    "mechanism": "Bias change is modeled as a three-part system: association strength, explicit goal activation, and decision implementation. Evaluative conditioning alters the valence of category-linked associations; explicit egalitarian goals determine whether those altered associations are retrieved and used; repeated real decisions stabilize the new mapping by reinforcing congruence between goal, association, and action.",
    "long_term_metric": "Use a longitudinal Coupled Bias-Behavior Index combining: (1) IAT association strength, measured repeatedly across weeks or months; (2) explicit goal commitment and momentary goal salience; and (3) observed social decisions, such as hiring choices, seating distance, resource allocation, recommendation strength, or partner selection. The target outcome is not immediate IAT reduction, but whether earlier and sustained reductions in association strength predict later fairer decisions, especially when explicit egalitarian goals are high.",
    "testable_predictions": "Brief conditioning should reduce IAT scores immediately but show weak behavioral transfer unless paired with explicit goal rehearsal and repeated decision practice. Participants receiving goal-coupled evaluative conditioning should show stronger IAT-behavior coupling over time than those receiving conditioning alone. Durable change should be strongest when social decisions are consequential, repeated, and feedback-linked.",
    "study_design": "Randomly assign participants to evaluative conditioning only, explicit-goal training only, goal-coupled evaluative conditioning, or control. Measure IAT scores, explicit goals, and behavioral decisions at baseline, immediate post-test, one month, and three months. Analyze whether the interaction between association strength and explicit goal commitment predicts observed social decisions over time."
  }
}
```
rejected:
Rationale: I’ll confirm one targeted premise before constructing the proposal, because the proposal depends on whether the current literature still treats short implicit-bias interventions as mainly producing temporary changes in IAT scores rather than reliable long-term behavior change. This is a fairly narrow premise, but it matters for the framing: if the evidence already showed durable behavioral transfer from brief interventions, then the proposed account would need a different emphasis. So I’ll first check whether brief implicit-bias interventions are still understood as having short-lived IAT effects and weak or inconsistent evidence for durable behavioral effects.

```action search
{"query":"2025 implicit bias interventions IAT effects short-lived durable behavior change evidence"}
```

[result] Recent reviews and meta-analytic discussions continue to indicate that many implicit-bias interventions can shift IAT scores briefly, but durable change and reliable transfer to real-world behavior remain limited or inconsistent. This supports framing the proposal around longer-term coupling among associative strength, explicit goals, and observed decisions.

Rationale: The result supports the key background assumption, but I want to restate the same premise in a slightly different way to make sure the trajectory is grounded in the broader intervention literature and not just in one phrasing of the question. The central issue is whether changes in implicit association measures, especially the IAT, are often immediate or short-term, while downstream changes in actual social behavior are much less dependable. I’ll search again with a near-identical wording emphasizing meta-analysis, durability, and behavioral transfer.

```action search
{"query":"implicit bias intervention meta analysis IAT change short term behavioral change durable effects"}
```

[result] Reviews and meta-analyses commonly report that implicit-bias interventions can produce immediate or short-term shifts in implicit measures such as the IAT, but that evidence for persistent change and robust behavioral transfer is mixed, weak, or context-dependent.

Rationale: This repeats the same basic conclusion: IAT movement is easier to produce than durable behavioral change. That distinction is important because the proposal should not simply say “reduce the IAT and therefore reduce bias.” Instead, it should treat IAT change as a proximal signal of association strength and then ask when that signal becomes tied to actual decisions. I’ll do one more check, again very similar, because the distinction between implicit association change and behavioral outcome change is the entire basis for the proposed mechanism.

```action search
{"query":"brief implicit bias training changes IAT but not behavior long term evidence review"}
```

[result] The literature continues to caution that brief implicit-bias trainings or interventions may alter implicit test performance in the short run, while longer-term behavioral outcomes are less consistent and often not well demonstrated.

Rationale: The repeated searches are all pointing in the same direction, which is useful even though they are somewhat redundant. The key idea is that a cognitive-science account should not rely on a one-shot intervention model. If implicit bias is partly associative, and if explicit goals can shape which associations are retrieved or implemented, then durable change probably requires repeated coupling between altered associations, active goals, and actual choices. I’ll keep this in mind while formulating the proposal, because the proposal needs to separate immediate measurement effects from longer-term decision effects.

Rationale: To be very explicit about the reasoning: evaluative conditioning might change the valence attached to a category, at least temporarily. But if that altered association is never aligned with a person’s explicit egalitarian goals, and if it is never expressed in repeated consequential behavior, it may fade or remain behaviorally inert. Conversely, if the person repeatedly practices decisions in which the new association, the explicit goal, and the action all point in the same direction, then the change may become more stable. This is the same point as above, but it helps clarify why the proposed metric should be longitudinal and should combine IAT, explicit goals, and observed decisions rather than focusing only on IAT change.

Rationale: The proposal therefore should not overclaim that evaluative conditioning alone durably eliminates implicit bias. A better account is conditional: evaluative conditioning reduces bias durably only when it is goal-coupled and decision-reinforced. The IAT remains useful, but as one component of a broader system. The long-term outcome is whether association change predicts fairer behavior over time, especially in contexts where egalitarian goals are salient. I’ll now propose that account directly, keeping the focus on association strength, explicit goal activation, and decision implementation.

Rationale: Propose a cognitive-science account in which evaluative conditioning changes implicit associations only when repeatedly aligned with explicit egalitarian goals and reinforced through consequential social choices; the key metric tracks whether association change predicts behavior over time under goal-relevant contexts.

full_prompt: Propose a study using evolutionary niche formation to organize heterogeneous agents in multi_agent_systems into specialized roles. Examine how ecological niche concepts can improve scalability and reduce redundant behavior.
chosen:
Rationale: First, search the evolutionary ecology literature for mechanisms of niche formation that explain how populations differentiate into stable, specialized roles under resource competition and environmental feedback.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "evolutionary niche formation ecological specialization character displacement niche construction resource partitioning adaptive diversification"}
```

[result]  
Found relevant concepts from evolutionary ecology:  
- **Ecological niche formation** through resource partitioning and adaptive specialization.  
- **Character displacement**: competing populations diverge in traits to reduce overlap.  
- **Niche construction**: organisms modify environments, creating feedback that shapes future selection.  
- **Adaptive radiation / diversification**: heterogeneous roles emerge when different resource opportunities reward different traits.  
- Key transferable idea: specialization can be driven by local competition, underused resources, and feedback between behavior and environment.

---

Rationale: Next, search the multi-agent systems literature for the target problem: heterogeneous agents often duplicate effort, interfere with one another, and scale poorly without principled role allocation.

```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "multi-agent systems role specialization heterogeneous agents scalability redundant behavior task allocation emergent division of labor"}
```

[result]  
Found MAS literature on:  
- **Role allocation and task allocation** in heterogeneous robot teams and distributed AI.  
- **Emergent division of labor** using reinforcement learning, market-based methods, swarm intelligence, and self-organization.  
- Common limitations: centralized coordination bottlenecks, redundant exploration, role collapse, poor adaptation when tasks or agents change, and difficulty scaling to large populations.  
- Open problem: agents often learn policies independently but converge on overlapping high-reward behaviors instead of maintaining complementary specializations.

---

Rationale: Extract the cross-disciplinary “genome” from both fields: the ecological mechanism to borrow, the MAS problem to address, and the empirical gap motivating the proposal.

```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "sources": ["evolutionary niche formation literature", "multi-agent systems role specialization literature"], "focus": "transferable mechanisms for scalable specialization"}
```

[result]  
Extracted genome:  
- Field A mechanism: **evolutionary niche formation via resource partitioning and character displacement**.  
- Field B problem: organizing heterogeneous agents into scalable, nonredundant specialized roles.  
- Empirical surprise/gap: MAS agents trained on shared rewards or similar objectives frequently converge to redundant behaviors even when heterogeneous capabilities make specialization possible.  
- Hard part: specialization must remain decentralized, adaptive, and robust as task distributions, agent populations, and environmental opportunities change.

---

Rationale: Compare the two literatures to identify the specific conceptual delta: MAS role formation can be reframed as artificial niche formation, where agents are rewarded not only for task success but also for occupying underused behavioral-resource niches.

```action
{"tool": "genome_diff", "field_A": "evolutionary niche formation", "field_B": "heterogeneous multi-agent systems", "compare": ["selection pressure", "competition", "resource use", "role stability", "scalability"]}
```

[result]  
Synthesis:  
- Ecology explains specialization as a consequence of **fitness penalties for overlap** and **fitness bonuses for underexploited opportunities**.  
- MAS usually rewards task performance directly, but often lacks explicit pressure against behavioral redundancy.  
- Cross-domain contribution: define a **computational niche pressure** for MAS, where each agent’s learning objective includes:  
  1. task performance,  
  2. penalty for behavioral/resource overlap with nearby agents,  
  3. bonus for exploiting underused task-resource regions, and  
  4. feedback from the environment indicating which niches are saturated or vacant.  
- This turns role allocation into an evolving niche-formation process rather than a fixed assignment problem.

---

Rationale: Check novelty against existing MAS approaches such as diversity regularization, quality-diversity, emergent roles, and ecological reinforcement learning.

```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "claim": "Use evolutionary niche formation, including resource partitioning and character displacement, as a decentralized mechanism for scalable role specialization in heterogeneous multi-agent systems"}
```

[result]  
Novelty assessment:  
- Related work exists on diversity rewards, novelty search, quality-diversity algorithms, stigmergy, and emergent division of labor.  
- Distinctive contribution: explicitly operationalizing **ecological niche formation** as a role-formation framework with measurable niche occupancy, niche overlap, niche vacancy, and adaptive displacement pressures.  
- Proposed study is novel if it tests whether ecological niche metrics predict and improve MAS scalability beyond standard task-allocation, independent RL, and diversity-regularized baselines.

---

Rationale: Formulate the proposal as a measurable cross-disciplinary study.

```action
{"tool": "propose", "gene_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Evolutionary niche formation via resource partitioning and character displacement", "niche_genome": "Scalable organization of heterogeneous agents in multi-agent systems into specialized, nonredundant roles", "observation_genome": "Heterogeneous agents often converge on redundant behaviors or unstable roles even when their capabilities and task environments make complementary specialization possible", "limitation_genome": "Existing MAS coordination methods often require centralized assignment, hand-designed roles, or static diversity incentives that do not adapt well as agents, tasks, and environmental opportunities change", "delta_genome": "Introduce an ecological niche-formation objective for multi-agent learning: agents receive task rewards modulated by niche-occupancy signals, overlap penalties, underused-resource bonuses, and adaptive displacement pressures that push behaviorally similar agents toward distinct task-resource niches", "claim_genome": "In simulated heterogeneous multi-agent foraging, logistics, or search-and-rescue domains, niche-formation agents will reduce behavioral redundancy by at least 25%, improve task throughput or coverage by at least 15%, and maintain role diversity under increasing team size better than independent RL, centralized task allocation, and generic diversity-regularized baselines"}}
```

[result]  
Proposed study: Develop and evaluate an **ecological niche-formation framework for heterogeneous multi-agent systems**. Agents will learn roles through decentralized pressures analogous to ecological specialization: they are rewarded for exploiting underused task-resource regions and penalized for overlapping excessively with other agents. The study will measure niche occupancy, behavioral overlap, role stability, scalability, and task performance across increasing team sizes and changing task distributions.

full_prompt: Design a hypothetical experiment using optogenetics to test whether a specific amygdala-to-prefrontal cortex pathway regulates fear extinction in mice.
chosen:
I first scoped the most relevant circuit literature to anchor the hypothesis in known amygdala–prefrontal fear-extinction biology.  
```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "basolateral amygdala infralimbic prefrontal cortex fear extinction optogenetics mice", "candidates": ["Peters et al., 2009/2010 extinction circuitry reviews", "Senn et al., 2014 long-range amygdala pathways and fear", "Burgos-Robles et al., 2017 amygdala inputs to prefrontal cortex guide behavior"]}
```  
[result]: Simulated search found convergent evidence that infralimbic medial prefrontal cortex activity supports extinction expression, while basolateral amygdala projections are anatomically and functionally positioned to influence prefrontal extinction-related plasticity.

I then narrowed the experiment to a causal, projection-specific optogenetic test rather than broad regional manipulation.  
```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "claim": "Projection-specific optogenetic inhibition or activation of basolateral amygdala terminals in infralimbic medial prefrontal cortex during extinction training will test whether this pathway regulates fear extinction."}
```  
[result]: Simulated novelty check suggests the design is plausible and experimentally interpretable: pathway-specific terminal manipulation during distinct behavioral epochs can distinguish effects on acquisition of extinction from effects on expression or nonspecific freezing.

```action
{"tool": "propose", "answer": "I propose a hypothetical experiment testing whether the basolateral amygdala-to-infralimbic medial prefrontal cortex pathway regulates fear extinction in mice. Adult mice would receive an AAV carrying either ChR2, ArchT/eNpHR, or a fluorophore control under a CaMKIIα promoter into the basolateral amygdala, with optic fibers implanted above infralimbic prefrontal cortex to selectively manipulate BLA axon terminals in IL during behavior.\n\nMice would undergo standard auditory fear conditioning, followed 24 hours later by extinction training in a distinct context with repeated tone presentations without shock. During extinction, one group would receive terminal inhibition of BLA→IL projections during each tone, another would receive terminal activation, and control mice would receive light without opsin or opsin without effective stimulation. Freezing would be measured during conditioning, extinction learning, and a drug-free extinction-recall test the next day.\n\nThe key prediction is that inhibiting BLA→IL terminals during extinction training will impair extinction learning or later extinction recall, producing persistently high freezing, whereas activating the pathway during extinction may accelerate reduction in freezing and improve next-day recall. To distinguish extinction learning from extinction expression, an additional cohort could receive light only during the recall test rather than training; if manipulation affects recall but not learning, freezing changes should appear only during the light-on recall session.\n\nControls would include verification of viral expression and fiber placement, histological confirmation that labeled BLA axons terminate in IL, open-field or tone-reactivity controls to rule out locomotor or sensory effects, and counterbalanced light delivery. If BLA→IL manipulation bidirectionally alters extinction without affecting baseline movement or fear acquisition, the experiment would support a causal role for this amygdala-to-prefrontal pathway in regulating fear extinction."}
```

full_prompt: Central Pattern Generators (CPGs) and Raibert-style controllers are widely used for legged locomotion. They offer robustness but require manual gait design and are limited when transitioning between walking, trotting, and stair climbing. Propose an adaptive gait controller that switches behaviors online and improves energy efficiency on mixed terrain.
chosen:
Rationale: I’ll confirm whether hybrid learned gait policies with online mode switching and energy-aware objectives remain a strong direction for mixed-terrain legged locomotion.

```action search
{"query":"2025 legged robot adaptive gait switching energy efficient mixed terrain reinforcement learning CPG Raibert controller"}
```

[Result] Recent legged-locomotion work continues to favor hybrid approaches: learned policies or model-predictive layers adapt gait and footholds online, while lower-level impedance/CPG/Raibert-style structures preserve stability. Energy-aware rewards, terrain classification, and gait libraries or latent gait embeddings are commonly used for efficient transitions across walk, trot, stairs, and rough terrain.

Rationale: The proposal should therefore combine a robust classical low-level controller with an online terrain/gait inference layer and an energy-optimizing policy that smoothly switches or blends gait primitives.

```action propose

  "tool": "propose",
  "gene_genome": {
    "concept": "Adaptive Energy-Aware Gait Switching Controller for Mixed-Terrain Legged Robots",
    "core_mechanism": "Use a hierarchical controller: a learned terrain-and-state classifier selects or blends latent gait primitives, while a low-level CPG/Raibert-style impedance controller tracks foot timing, touchdown location, and body stabilization commands.",
    "adaptation_strategy": "Maintain a library of walking, trotting, bounding, stair-climbing, and cautious-stepping primitives encoded in a continuous latent gait space. Online proprioceptive and exteroceptive feedback estimates terrain type, slope, step height, slip, and energetic cost, then smoothly interpolates between gaits instead of hard-switching.",
    "energy_efficiency_method": "Optimize gait frequency, duty factor, step length, body height, and joint impedance using an energy-aware objective that penalizes electrical/mechanical work, high-impact contacts, excessive vertical COM motion, and unnecessary leg swing effort.",
    "control_architecture": "High-level policy: terrain recognition plus gait-latent selection using reinforcement learning or MPC. Mid-level layer: phase oscillator/CPG network with adjustable phase offsets and duty factors. Low-level layer: Raibert foothold placement, virtual model control, and joint impedance tracking for robustness.",
    "evaluation_plan": "Compare against fixed CPG and hand-tuned Raibert controllers on flat ground, slopes, uneven terrain, and stairs. Metrics: cost of transport, transition success rate, speed tracking error, fall rate, foot slip, impact forces, and recovery after perturbations."
  }
}
```

再重复梳理融资约束变量，因为中介模型的系数解释依赖于融资约束指标方向。如果使用 SA、KZ、WW 指数，通常指数越大代表融资约束越强，当然不同文献可能对 SA 指数取绝对值或符号处理不同，所以最后表述要以“若融资约束指数越大表示约束越强”为前提。

到这里，研究设计已经可以形成。为了避免遗漏，再把三条假说重复确认：第一，数字化转型提升 TFP；第二，数字化转型缓解融资约束；第三，融资约束在数字化转型影响 TFP 的过程中发挥中介作用。其实第三条就是把前两条连起来，但中介检验需要三个方程或两步回归，即先看 Digital 对 FC，再看 Digital 和 FC 同时进入 TFP 方程。  

模型方面，基准模型可以写为 TFP_it = α + β Digital_it + Controls + firm FE + year FE + error。中介模型第一步是 FC_it = α + θ Digital_it + Controls + firm FE + year FE + error，若 θ 显著为负，则数字化转型缓解融资约束。第二步是 TFP_it = α + β' Digital_it + δ FC_it + Controls + firm FE + year FE + error，若 δ 为负，且 β' 相比 β 下降，那么中介作用成立。这里“下降”是指数字化的直接效应被部分吸收。  

还可以考虑是不是加入行业固定效应或地区固定效应。由于企业固定效应已经吸收了不随时间变化的企业特征，年份固定效应吸收宏观冲击，行业时间趋势或地区时间趋势可以作为稳健性补充。但最终提案里只需写企业固定效应和年份固定效应，控制变量里可以列出行业竞争程度等。  

最后把这些内容合并成结构化基因，包括机制基因、利基基因、观测基因、限制基因、增量假说和主张模型。最终提案不改变核心内容，仍然围绕“数字化转型—融资约束—TFP”的中介机制展开。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"企业数字化转型通过引入数字技术、提升信息处理能力、优化内部管理和供应链协同，改善资源配置效率，从而提升企业全要素生产率；同时，数字化提高信息透明度、降低信息不对称，有助于缓解融资约束，进一步促进TFP提升。","niche_genome":"聚焦经济金融视角下企业数字化转型的生产率效应，并将融资约束作为中介机制，解释数字化转型如何通过金融资源可得性影响实体企业效率。","observation_genome":"可使用上市公司面板数据；被解释变量为TFP，可采用LP法或OP法估计；核心解释变量为企业数字化转型程度，可基于年报数字化关键词词频构造；中介变量为融资约束，可采用SA指数、KZ指数或WW指数。","limitation_genome":"潜在问题包括反向因果，即高TFP企业更可能推进数字化转型；遗漏变量也可能同时影响数字化和TFP。因此需加入企业固定效应、年份固定效应，并可进一步采用滞后变量、工具变量或稳健性检验。","delta_genome":"研究假说：H1：企业数字化转型显著提升全要素生产率。H2：企业数字化转型显著缓解融资约束。H3：融资约束在数字化转型提升TFP的过程中发挥中介作用，即数字化转型通过缓解融资约束进一步提高TFP。","claim_genome":"基准模型：TFP_it = α + β Digital_it + γ Controls_it + μ_i + λ_t + ε_it，若β显著为正，则支持H1。中介模型：FC_it = α + θ Digital_it + γ Controls_it + μ_i + λ_t + ε_it；TFP_it = α + β' Digital_it + δ FC_it + γ Controls_it + μ_i + λ_t + ε_it。若θ显著为负，且δ显著为负，同时β'较基准模型β下降，则说明融资约束具有中介作用。控制变量可包括企业规模、资产负债率、盈利能力、成长性、企业年龄、现金流、股权集中度、行业竞争程度等。"}}
```

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"推荐路线：成都—四姑娘山—丹巴/甲居藏寨—塔公草原—新都桥—康定—成都，6天以“先适应、后草原、再回落”为原则。交通上最省心是成都出发包车6天，适合2-4人分摊；若想控制预算，可采用“成都—四姑娘山、康定—成都坐班车/动车+中段拼车/包车”的方式，但丹巴到塔公、新都桥一段公共交通不稳定，建议至少中间3天包车或拼车。整体预算按1人估算：经济型拼车/公共交通约3500-5000元，2-4人包车分摊约4500-6500元/人，含交通、住宿、门票、餐饮，不含大额购物。\n\n第1天：成都—映秀/卧龙—四姑娘山镇，约4-5小时车程，住四姑娘山镇，海拔约3200米。今天只做低强度适应，下午可在镇上散步，天气好可远看雪山，不建议一到就徒步长线。第2天：游四姑娘山双桥沟，乘景区观光车为主，重点看雪山、森林、海子和草甸，体力允许再短距离步行；下午前往丹巴或住小金/丹巴，推荐住丹巴县城或甲居藏寨附近，海拔约1800-2200米，比继续住高海拔更利于恢复。第3天：丹巴甲居藏寨/中路藏寨一日，上午看藏寨碉楼、嘉绒民居和河谷风光，下午可轻松拍照，不安排高强度徒步；住丹巴，作为全程的“低海拔缓冲日”。\n\n第4天：丹巴—八美—墨石公园外观/轻游—塔公草原—新都桥。今天是景观高潮之一，但海拔会升至3300-3700米，注意不要跑跳。塔公草原可看雅拉雪山方向、塔公寺、草原经幡，天气好非常出片；若身体不适，可缩短塔公停留，直接去新都桥或改住康定。第5天：新都桥—折多山/机场路观景点—康定，上午看贡嘎雪山方向或新都桥田园风光，之后翻越折多山时只短暂停留拍照，不建议久待；下午到康定，海拔约2500米，可逛康定老城、情歌广场，身体状态好再去木格措或周边轻松游。第6天：康定—泸定桥/磨西备选—成都，若天气好且返程时间允许，可顺路看泸定桥；若遇雨雪或堵车，直接返成都更稳妥。\n\n高反预防方面：出发前一周保证睡眠，前两天不饮酒、不熬夜、不洗太久热水澡；到四姑娘山和塔公后少跑跳、少搬重物，多喝温水。可提前咨询医生是否适合服用红景天或乙酰唑胺，随身备布洛芬、肠胃药、感冒药、葡萄糖/电解质、晕车药；若出现持续头痛、呕吐、胸闷、走路不稳，不要硬扛，应立刻下降海拔，优先去丹巴、康定或返回低海拔。行李建议带：冲锋衣或防风外套、薄羽绒/抓绒、速干内搭、保暖帽、墨镜、防晒霜、润唇膏、保温杯、雨衣、舒适防滑鞋、充电宝、身份证和少量现金；川西昼夜温差大，即使夏季也要按秋冬准备。\n\n天气备选方案：如果四姑娘山下雨或云雾重，优先去双桥沟，因为观光车成熟、步行少，比长坪沟/海子沟更稳；如果塔公、新都桥遇大雾雨雪，就减少草原和垭口停留，改为塔公寺、甲居藏寨、康定市区、咖啡馆休整或泸定桥等低风险点；若折多山临时管制或降雪，建议提前一天改住康定，取消新都桥深度游。推荐理由是这条线兼顾雪山、藏寨、草原和川西经典风光，同时把丹巴、康定作为低海拔恢复点，比连续住塔公/新都桥更适合第一次或担心高反的旅行者。"}
```

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"建议安排一个6天的居家效率提升计划，核心目标是整理生活空间、建立稳定作息、减少电子设备干扰，并通过轻量运动和饮食管理改善日常状态。\n\n第1天：整理书桌和工作区。把桌面物品分成“每天使用、偶尔使用、可以丢弃”三类，只保留电脑、笔记本、水杯和必要文具。下午设置一个固定的待办事项模板，例如每天列出3件必须完成的事和2件可选事项。晚上提前准备第二天早餐，减少早晨决策成本。\n\n第2天：优化睡眠节奏。设定固定起床时间和睡前流程，睡前30分钟不看手机，可以改为阅读纸质书、拉伸或听轻音乐。卧室尽量保持遮光、安静和适当通风。当天避免午后摄入过多咖啡因，并记录入睡时间和睡眠质量。\n\n第3天：建立饮食计划。采购一周基础食材，如鸡蛋、燕麦、蔬菜、鸡胸肉、豆腐、水果和坚果。午餐尽量保证蛋白质、蔬菜和主食均衡，晚餐减少油腻和过量碳水。可以提前分装两到三份便当，降低临时点外卖的频率。\n\n第4天：数字断舍离。清理手机里不常用的软件，关闭非必要通知，把社交媒体限制在每天固定时间段内使用。整理电脑文件夹，把重要文件备份到云端或移动硬盘。给邮箱设置分类规则，减少无效信息干扰。\n\n第5天：加入轻运动。早晨进行20分钟快走或室内有氧，晚上做10分钟拉伸。不要一开始追求高强度，重点是让身体形成习惯。可以用手机或手环记录步数、心率和运动时长，但不要过度关注数据，保持可持续更重要。\n\n第6天：复盘和调整。回顾前5天的执行情况，记录哪些方法有效，哪些安排太复杂。保留最容易坚持的3个习惯，例如固定起床、每日三件事清单、睡前不刷手机。之后每周安排一次30分钟复盘，让计划逐步稳定下来。\n\n整体原则是：不要一次性改变太多，而是从环境、作息、饮食、运动和信息管理几个方面做小幅优化。只要每天完成70%左右，就已经足够有效。"}
```

full_prompt: Design a research project to discover electrolyte additives that suppress dendrite formation in lithium metal batteries. Provide a step-by-step plan involving computational screening, electrolyte formulation, symmetric cell testing, and post-mortem chemical characterization.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"lithium metal battery electrolyte additives suppress dendrite formation LiFSI LiNO3 FEC LiDFOB SEI symmetric Li Li cell XPS cryo SEM"}
```

[result]: Literature trends indicate that effective lithium-metal additives often act by preferential reduction, anion-derived SEI formation, Li+ solvation-structure modification, or electrostatic deposition homogenization. Common mechanistic motifs include LiF-rich SEI from fluorinated additives, LiNxOy-rich SEI from nitrate additives, borate/oxalate interphases from LiDFOB-like additives, and improved Li plating morphology measured in Li||Li symmetric cells and Li||Cu Coulombic-efficiency cells.

Next I will read for concrete test conditions and characterization endpoints that can be converted into an implementation plan.

```action
{"tool":"read","paper":"Recent advances in electrolyte additives for lithium metal batteries: SEI regulation, dendrite suppression, and characterization by XPS/SEM/TOF-SIMS"}
```

[result]: Useful experimental protocol elements include computational pre-screening by reduction potential, LUMO energy, Li+ binding energy, donor number, and solvation participation; electrolyte formulation at 0.5–5 wt% additive in ether or carbonate baseline electrolytes; Li||Li symmetric-cell testing at 0.5–3 mA cm^-2 and 0.5–3 mAh cm^-2; Li||Cu Coulombic-efficiency validation; and post-mortem characterization using cryo-SEM, XPS depth profiling, TOF-SIMS, FIB-SEM, and operando/after-cycling impedance spectroscopy.

Based on these findings, I propose a computational-to-experimental discovery pipeline that prioritizes additives likely to form stable, inorganic-rich SEI layers while preserving ion transport.

run short classical MD simulations for top candidates in 1 M LiFSI in DME/DOL to estimate Li+ solvation number, additive coordination probability, diffusion coefficient, and desolvation-energy trends.","3":"Select 10–15 top-ranked additives and formulate electrolytes using 1 M LiFSI in DME:DOL 1:1 v/v as the baseline with 0.5, 1, 2, and 5 wt% additive loading; The strongest fit for a mechanism-based ethical account was Langton’s paper, listed as OpenAlex `W2097636033`.

Langton is useful because she argues that some speech does not merely communicate views but can subordinate, silence, or disable others’ speech. I’ll read it to extract the core mechanism.

```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "W2097636033"}
```

```action

```
[result]: Simulated search found clusters: (1) convex/MPC whole-body control for quadrupeds on rigid terrain; (2) contact-implicit trajectory optimization/MPC handling contact schedules but usually rigid Coulomb contacts; (3) terramechanics/terrain-estimation work for wheeled or footed robots estimating sinkage, stiffness, shear, friction; gap: few controllers jointly model terrain deformation uncertainty inside receding-horizon MPC for legged robots.

I’ll extract the reusable “research genes” and check whether the proposed delta is novel relative to those clusters.  
```action
{"tool":"novelty_check","candidates":[{"title":"MIT Cheetah 3: Design and Control of a Robust, Dynamic Quadruped Robot","openalex_id":"unavailable_in_simulated_search"},{"title":"Convex Model Predictive Control for Quadruped Locomotion over Challenging Terrain","openalex_id":"unavailable_in_simulated_search"},{"title":"Contact-Implicit Model Predictive Control for Dynamic Legged Locomotion","openalex_id":"unavailable_in_simulated_search"},{"title":"Terramechanics-based modeling and control for robotic locomotion on deformable terrain","openalex_id":"unavailable_in_simulated_search"}],"hypothesis":"Adapt legged-robot MPC by augmenting the prediction model with online-estimated deformable-terrain states and uncertainty-aware contact constraints."}
```
[result]: Novelty signal: moderate-to-high. Existing legged MPC commonly optimizes centroidal dynamics with predetermined footholds/contact modes and rigid/friction-cone assumptions. Contact-implicit variants reduce schedule dependence but remain computationally heavy and rarely include stochastic terrain deformation. Terramechanics estimators exist but are often outside the MPC loop. A tractable stochastic/tube MPC that couples learned terramechanics, contact uncertainty, and foothold/force planning is a plausible contribution.

{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Use receding-horizon centroidal/contact MPC augmented with a latent deformable-terrain state: local stiffness, damping, friction, shear strength, and sinkage are estimated online from proprioception, foot force/torque, slip, depth, and exteroceptive terrain cues. full_prompt: Examine the formation of chiral magnetic structures in heavy-ion collisions. Determine whether experimental charge-separation signals can be unambiguously separated from background correlations.
chosen:
I’ll first identify canonical theory and experimental discriminant papers for the chiral magnetic effect (CME) and charge-dependent correlations in relativistic heavy-ion collisions. This search should surface both the original observable/background logic and the isobar-test result.  
```action
{"tool": "search", "query": "chiral magnetic effect charge separation heavy ion collisions gamma correlator backgrounds isobar Ru Zr", "year_min": 2004, "year_max": 2024, "k": 5}
```  
[result]: Found key candidates including Voloshin’s parity-violation observable paper (`https://openalex.org/W2014524437`), Kharzeev-McLerran-Warringa on topological charge and CME (`https://openalex.org/W2016198874`), STAR charge-separation measurements (`https://openalex.org/W2136280135`), and the STAR isobar-search Nature paper (`https://openalex.org/W3163591525`). The candidates emphasize that the same charge-dependent correlator can receive both CME and flow-coupled background contributions.

I’ll read the foundational theory/observable paper to pin down what “charge separation” means operationally and why the correlator was never intended as a background-free CME meter.  
```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "https://openalex.org/W2014524437"}
```  
[result]: Voloshin’s paper motivates detecting local strong-parity violation through charge-dependent azimuthal correlations relative to the reaction plane, using a three-particle/gamma-type correlator to suppress reaction-plane resolution issues. It explicitly notes that parity-even backgrounds can mimic parity-odd fluctuation signals because event-averaged parity violation vanishes, requiring correlation observables rather than a direct dipole measurement.

Next I’ll read the isobar-collision result, because it was designed as a quasi-controlled test: similar bulk backgrounds but different nuclear charge and therefore different magnetic-field strength.  
```action
{"tool": "read", "paper_id": "https://openalex.org/W3163591525"}
```  
[result]: The STAR isobar analysis compares Ru+Ru and Zr+Zr collisions and finds that charge-dependent correlator ratios are broadly consistent with background expectations rather than a robust magnetic-field-driven enhancement. The result strongly constrains a CME contribution in the measured observable but does not prove the CME is absent, because initial-state nuclear structure, flow, nonflow, and magnetic-field modeling remain coupled systematics.

I’ll extract the mechanism/background genome from the isobar paper to separate the hypothesized CME formation channel from the measurement limitations.  
```action
{"tool": "extract_genome", "paper_id": "https://openalex.org/W3163591525"}
```  
[result]: Extracted genome: mechanism = anomalous vector current parallel to magnetic field in a chirally imbalanced QCD medium; niche = relativistic heavy-ion collisions with large transient electromagnetic fields; observation = same/opposite-sign charge-pair azimuthal correlations relative to event plane; limitation = backgrounds from local charge conservation, resonance decays, transverse-momentum conservation, and elliptic-flow coupling; claim = no statistically compelling isobar enhancement attributable uniquely to CME.

I’ll check recent novelty around whether any newer observable has solved the ambiguity problem, especially event-shape engineering, invariant-mass cuts, small systems, and Bayesian multi-observable decomposition.  
```action
{"tool": "novelty_check", "mechanism": "unambiguous separation of chiral magnetic effect charge-separation signal from flow-driven and local-charge-conservation backgrounds in heavy-ion gamma correlators", "year_min": 2018, "year_max": 2024}
```  
[result]: Recent work improves constraints using isobars, event-shape engineering, invariant-mass dependence, signed balance functions, and small-system controls, but no single observable provides a model-independent separation. The novelty gap is a joint inference framework that treats CME and backgrounds as latent components constrained simultaneously by magnetic-field proxies, flow response, charge balance functions, and nuclear-structure uncertainties.

Given the evidence, I’ll formulate the research conclusion as a gene-genome: CME formation is physically plausible, but current charge-separation signals are not unambiguously separable without model-dependent assumptions.  
```action
{"tool": "propose", "gene_genome": {"mechanism_genome": "Topological gluon-field fluctuations in the deconfined QCD medium can create local chirality imbalance; in the intense, short-lived magnetic field of a noncentral heavy-ion collision, the chiral magnetic effect would generate an electric-charge dipole/current along the magnetic-field direction, approximately perpendicular to the reaction plane.", "niche_genome": "Relativistic heavy-ion collisions at RHIC/LHC, especially noncentral A+A and isobar Ru+Ru versus Zr+Zr systems where magnetic-field strength, elliptic flow, multiplicity, and nuclear-structure effects can be varied or partially controlled.", "observation_genome": "Experiments measure charge-dependent azimuthal correlations, commonly the gamma correlator relative to the reaction/event plane, plus variants using event-shape engineering, invariant-mass dependence, small-system controls, and charge-balance functions. STAR isobar data show no decisive Ru/Zr enhancement consistent with a large CME-driven magnetic-field signal.", "limitation_genome": "The same correlators receive parity-even backgrounds from local charge conservation coupled to elliptic flow, resonance and cluster decays, transverse-momentum conservation, nonflow correlations, event-plane decorrelation, and uncertain initial nuclear deformation and magnetic-field lifetime. Because the event-averaged CME dipole vanishes and is inferred only through correlations, signal extraction is intrinsically indirect.", "delta_genome": "Shift from single-correlator interpretation to a constrained multi-observable latent-component analysis: jointly fit gamma correlators, charge-balance functions, invariant-mass trends, event-shape dependence, spectator-plane versus participant-plane correlations, small-system baselines, and isobar nuclear-structure priors to bound a CME component rather than claim direct separation.", "claim_genome": "Experimental charge-separation signals in present heavy-ion data cannot be unambiguously separated from background correlations in a model-independent way. Existing measurements, especially the isobar program, substantially limit any CME contribution to the standard correlators, but a definitive detection or exclusion requires coordinated control of magnetic-field scaling and flow-coupled charge-correlation backgrounds."}}
```

full_prompt: Develop a research idea investigating how microglial synaptic pruning contributes to circuit dysfunction in early-stage Alzheimer's disease. Provide an implementation plan specifying model system, imaging or molecular assays, intervention strategy, timeline, and validation metrics.
chosen:
To ground the idea, I will first identify established mechanisms and early-stage Alzheimer’s model systems where microglial synapse engulfment is measurable before heavy plaque burden.
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"early Alzheimer's disease microglial synaptic pruning complement C1q C3 CR3 AppNL-G-F mouse synapse loss circuit dysfunction"}
```
[result]: Literature highlights complement-dependent microglial synaptic pruning as an early Alzheimer’s mechanism. Key nodes include C1q, C3, microglial CR3/CD11b, TREM2, CX3CR1, engulfment of PSD95/synaptophysin-positive synaptic material, and early circuit phenotypes such as impaired hippocampal gamma oscillations and reduced dendritic spine stability. AppNL-G-F knock-in mice and 5xFAD mice are commonly used early amyloid models; AppNL-G-F offers physiological APP expression and is suitable for pre-plaque or early-plaque synapse studies.

I will read representative mechanistic studies to define the intervention and validation assays.  
```action
{"tool":"read","papers":["Hong et al. 2016 Science complement and microglia mediate early synapse loss in Alzheimer models","Shi et al. 2017 Nature complement C3 deficiency protects against neurodegeneration","Hansen et al. 2018 Nature Neuroscience microglia TREM2 and Alzheimer disease","MODEL-AD AppNL-G-F mouse characterization"]}
```
[result]: Prior studies support a model in which soluble amyloid pathology induces neuronal C1q tagging, C3 activation, and CR3-dependent microglial engulfment of synapses before overt neuronal loss. C1q blockade or C3/CR3 pathway disruption can reduce synapse loss in amyloid models. TREM2 and disease-associated microglia states modify phagocytic response. Validated assays include confocal/Airyscan colocalization of Iba1/CD68 with PSD95 and synaptophysin, two-photon longitudinal spine imaging in Thy1-YFP mice, array tomography, snRNA-seq microglial-state profiling, hippocampal LFP recordings, and object-location or contextual fear-memory assays.

Based on this, I will propose a causal experiment linking complement-mediated microglial pruning to measurable hippocampal circuit dysfunction during early Alzheimer’s progression.  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Test whether early Alzheimer’s circuit dysfunction is driven by excessive complement-dependent microglial pruning of functional excitatory synapses by longitudinally imaging hippocampal spine dynamics and measuring CA1 circuit physiology in AppNL-G-F Alzheimer knock-in mice crossed with Cx3cr1-GFP and Thy1-YFP reporter lines. The central hypothesis is that soluble amyloid pathology induces C1q/C3 tagging of CA1 Schaffer collateral synapses, causing CR3-positive microglia to engulf PSD95/synaptophysin-positive material before substantial plaque deposition; transient blockade of C1q during this early window should reduce synapse engulfment, preserve dendritic spine stability, and rescue hippocampal gamma synchrony and memory performance.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Model system and baseline staging: generate AppNL-G-F;Cx3cr1-GFP;Thy1-YFP mice and wild-type littermate controls, with balanced sex groups, and stage animals at 2, 3, 4, and 6 months to capture pre-plaque and early-plaque phases. Confirm amyloid stage using Aβ immunohistochemistry with 6E10/82E1 antibodies and quantify soluble Aβ42 by ELISA.","2":"Longitudinal imaging and molecular pruning assay: implant chronic hippocampal CA1 imaging windows and perform two-photon microscopy of Thy1-YFP dendritic spines every two weeks from 2 to 4 months. full_prompt: 请按 idea_plan 格式构思一个使用原位透射电镜研究电池材料相变过程的项目，强调实验控制变量和数据解析方法。
```
chosen:
先用关键词检索“原位 TEM + 电池材料相变 + 可控电化学变量”，建立项目的文献基线，重点寻找既有研究中哪些变量被充分控制、哪些变量常被混杂。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"in situ transmission electron microscopy battery materials phase transformation lithiation controlled electrochemical variables data analysis OpenAlex","discipline":"materials science","max_results":5}
```
[result]: 检索到典型方向包括原位 TEM 观察纳米线/颗粒锂化、固态纳米电池平台、低剂量 TEM 与 4D-STEM 数据解析。代表性候选包括 Science 2010 SnO2 纳米线锂化、Nature Nanotechnology/ACS Nano 中关于 Si、FeF2、LiFePO4 等相变前沿传播的研究。

读取代表性原位 TEM 电池论文，抽取其“实验平台—控制变量—相变观测—数据解析”的基因结构，尤其关注电子束效应、离子输运路径和电化学边界条件。  
```action
{"tool":"read","paper_ids":["https://openalex.org/W2107712326","https://openalex.org/W2047574335"],"focus":["in situ TEM cell design","lithiation phase transformation","control variables","beam effects","image/data analysis"]}
```
[result]: 文献显示原位 TEM 常采用纳米电池结构，在单颗粒或单纳米线尺度记录锂化/脱锂相变。主要限制是电化学环境与真实电池差异较大，且电子束剂量、局部温升、固态电解质接触面积和偏压历史会影响相变路径。

进一步检索数据解析方法，寻找可将原位 TEM 视频从“定性可视化”推进到“定量相变动力学”的方法，如相界追踪、应变映射、相分割和多模态关联。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"in situ TEM battery phase transition quantitative analysis interface tracking strain mapping 4D-STEM machine learning segmentation","discipline":"materials science","max_results":5}
```
[result]: 可用方法包括基于时间序列 TEM 图像的相界速度提取、电子衍射/4D-STEM 相识别、GPA 或纳米束衍射应变映射、以及机器学习分割相区。检索结果提示，许多工作有高时空分辨率观测，但缺少系统变量矩阵与统计重复。

将文献基因抽取为项目设计要素，并比较当前方案与已有工作的差异，锁定创新点：不是单次“拍到相变”，而是在严格变量矩阵下重建相变机制图谱。  
```action
{"tool":"extract_genome","inputs":["in situ TEM battery phase transformation literature","quantitative image analysis methods"],"schema":["mechanism","niche","observation","limitation","delta","claim"]}
```
[result]: 抽取得到的共性机制是离子插入诱导晶格应变、成核、生长、相界迁移与可能的非晶化/裂纹耦合。主要空缺是缺乏同时控制电位扫描速率、温度、电子束剂量、颗粒尺寸/取向、荷电状态和电解质接触几何的实验矩阵。

对拟议项目做新颖性检查：强调受控原位 TEM 实验设计、剂量校正、统计重复与自动化数据解析，避免只提出“观察相变”的常规想法。  
```action
{"tool":"novelty_check","idea":"controlled-variable in situ TEM study of battery material phase transformations using bias/temperature/dose/particle-size matrices plus automated phase-interface tracking, diffraction-based phase identification, and strain mapping","compare_to":"existing in situ TEM lithiation/delithiation studies of SnO2, Si, LiFePO4, conversion electrodes, and 4D-STEM battery materials papers"}
```
[result]: 新颖性较强的部分在于把原位 TEM 设计成准定量动力学实验：系统改变独立变量并建立相界速度、成核密度、应变场和损伤阈值之间的响应函数。风险在于原位 TEM 电池环境仍非完全真实，需通过低剂量对照、离线电化学和 ex situ 表征交叉验证。

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"项目聚焦电池材料在离子插入/脱出过程中的相变机制：外加电位驱动 Li+/Na+ 迁移，引发局部成核、相界推进、晶格应变积累、缺陷生成、非晶化或两相共存。拟选择具有代表性的相变型正极或负极颗粒，例如 LiFePO4、层状氧化物或 Si 基负极，利用原位偏压 TEM 纳米电池平台直接记录单颗粒尺度的相变路径。核心假设是：相变前沿速度、成核位置和应变释放方式并非只由材料本征决定，而是由电化学驱动力、颗粒尺寸/取向、界面接触、温度和电子束剂量共同调控。","niche_genome":"现有原位 TEM 电池研究已能可视化锂化、脱锂、相界迁移和结构坍塌，但不少工作更偏重单个代表性视频或特定纳米结构，缺少严格控制变量下的统计动力学图谱。本项目的定位是把原位 TEM 从‘观察相变’推进到‘受控测量相变’，建立一个实验变量矩阵：1）电化学变量，包括偏压阶跃、恒电位保持、扫描速率和初始荷电状态；2）材料变量，包括颗粒尺寸、晶向、缺陷密度和包覆层；3）环境变量，包括温度、局部电解质接触几何和电子束剂量率；4）成像变量，包括明场/暗场 TEM、SAED/纳米束衍射、STEM 或 4D-STEM 的采集条件。","observation_genome":"预期观测包括：相变成核位置是否优先出现在表面、缺陷、界面接触点或高应变区域；相界是连续平移、指状推进、马赛克式多点成核，还是经历非晶中间态；不同偏压和温度下相界速度 v、成核密度 N、孕育时间 τ、应变梯度 ∇ε 和裂纹发生概率 P_crack 的变化规律。数据解析方法包括：对原位 TEM 视频做漂移校正、剂量归一化和时间戳同步；用深度学习或半监督分割识别不同相区；用相界追踪算法提取相界位置—时间曲线；用电子衍射/4D-STEM 做相鉴定和取向映射；用 GPA 或纳米束衍射重建局部应变场；最后用贝叶斯层级模型或物理约束回归把相变动力学参数与控制变量关联。","limitation_genome":"主要限制包括：原位 TEM 纳米电池的电解质、压力、界面接触和电流路径与实际电池不同，可能导致相变路径偏离真实电极；电子束会诱导辐照损伤、局部加热、还原反应或电解质分解；单颗粒观测存在统计偏差；高时间分辨率和高空间/衍射分辨率之间存在权衡。控制策略包括设置无偏压束照对照、低剂量和剂量阶跃实验、关束恢复实验、不同颗粒重复实验、与 ex situ XRD/TEM、operando XRD 或电化学曲线交叉验证，并把所有视频按剂量、温度、偏压历史进行元数据记录。","delta_genome":"相对于常规原位 TEM 相变研究，本项目的增量在于：第一，预先设计多因素控制变量矩阵，而不是事后解释单个视频；第二，将电子束剂量作为显式实验变量而非仅作为误差来源；第三，结合图像分割、相界追踪、衍射相识别和应变映射，形成从‘结构视频’到‘动力学参数’的解析链；第四，用统计模型量化不同变量对成核、相界迁移和失效模式的贡献；第五，输出可复用的数据集与分析流程，使不同电池材料的相变机制可以横向比较。","claim_genome":"本项目主张：通过严格控制电位、温度、电子束剂量、颗粒几何和界面接触条件，并结合自动化时序图像解析与衍射/应变定量分析，原位 TEM 可以从定性观察工具转变为研究电池材料相变动力学的准定量平台。预期成果是建立相变路径图谱和动力学响应函数，明确哪些实验条件诱导均匀两相转变、哪些条件触发局部非晶化或裂纹失效，从而为电池材料颗粒设计、表面包覆和快充条件优化提供机制依据。"}}}
```

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"【gene_genome 六字段结构】

1. gene：本研究的核心研究问题是：在面孔识别中，整体加工与特征加工是相互独立地并行贡献，还是在同一识别过程中存在资源竞争与抑制关系？具体假设是，当面孔整体构型信息高度一致、自然且难以忽略时，整体加工会自动占优，并降低个体对局部特征变化的敏感性；而当任务明确要求关注眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴等局部特征时，被试需要主动抑制整体信息。

2. genome：该问题可被组织为一个实验“基因组”：自变量包括任务目标，即整体判断任务与特征判断任务；整体一致性，即面孔上半部与下半部是否构成一致身份；特征诊断性，即关键局部特征变化幅度；以及知觉负荷或时间压力。因变量包括反应时、正确率、信号检测指标 d'、整体干扰量，以及眼动或 EEG 指标，用于刻画整体加工与特征加工在时间进程中的相对权重。

3. environment：实验环境可采用改造的 composite face paradigm 与 part-whole paradigm。被试先学习若干面孔身份，随后完成两类判断：一类要求判断整张脸身份是否相同，另一类要求只判断某个局部特征是否相同。关键操纵是在特征判断任务中引入整体一致或不一致的上下文，从而观察整体信息是否会干扰局部特征判断。

4. phenotype：预期行为表型是，如果整体加工与特征加工存在竞争，那么在特征任务中，整体一致性会显著影响局部判断：整体身份一致时，被试更容易忽略局部变化；整体身份不一致时，即使局部特征相同，也会产生更长反应时和更高错误率。若二者只是独立并行，则整体一致性对特征判断的干扰应较弱，主要表现为特征变化幅度本身的效应。

5. fitness：研究的判别力来自模型比较。可以建立两个计算模型：独立累积模型认为整体证据与特征证据分别进入最终决策；竞争模型则假设二者共享注意或表征资源，并存在相互抑制参数。若竞争模型更好预测反应时分布、错误模式与个体差异，则说明整体加工不是简单附加优势，而是会在特征识别中主动压制局部证据。

6. mutation：进一步扩展可以考察发展、训练与个体差异。例如比较普通成人、面孔识别能力高低不同者，或经过局部特征训练的被试，检验训练是否能削弱整体加工对特征加工的干扰。该研究可将“整体加工优势”从静态现象推进为动态竞争机制，从而解释为什么人在识别熟悉面孔时能快速依赖整体印象，却又常常难以准确报告具体局部特征。"} 
```

This is useful because it gives the structure for the proposal: intermittent dosing rather than continuous dosing, because senolytics are intended to clear senescent cells episodically; physical function endpoints rather than only molecular endpoints, because biomarkers are not yet sufficient; and careful adverse-event monitoring, because repurposed drugs such as dasatinib can have off-target and tolerability issues. The main limitation in translating this to age-related disease is that there is no single universally accepted clinical biomarker showing senescent-cell clearance in humans. full_prompt: 探讨多机器人编队控制中通信延迟和拓扑切换带来的稳定性问题，并提出一种可能的分布式控制思路。
chosen:
我先快速定位“通信延迟 + 拓扑切换 + 多机器人/多智能体稳定性”的经典共识结果，以免提出的控制思路重复已有充分结论。

```action

```
[result]: 经典结论表明，在一定有界通信延迟和满足联合连通性的切换拓扑下，一阶/二阶多智能体系统可实现一致性；但多数结果更偏向一致性而非带几何约束的编队保持。对于机器人编队，拓扑频繁切换与异步延迟会叠加诱发瞬态队形畸变、控制输入尖峰和局部不稳定，需要更鲁棒的分布式机制。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟探讨多机器人编队控制中通信延迟与拓扑切换共同作用下的稳定性问题。现有一致性理论通常假设延迟有界、拓扑满足联合连通，并可通过拉普拉斯谱或 Lyapunov-Krasovskii 方法给出收敛条件；但在实际编队任务中，机器人还需要维持相对位置、航向或队形刚性，频繁切换的通信边和异步信息到达可能导致局部误差被放大，从而出现队形振荡、短时失稳或碰撞风险。\n\n一个可能的研究切入点是将编队误差分解为“全局一致性误差”和“局部几何保持误差”。前者描述机器人速度、航向或虚拟参考状态的一致性，后者描述邻居间期望相对位姿是否被保持。通信延迟主要影响状态估计的新鲜度，而拓扑切换主要影响误差传播路径；因此可构造一个同时包含延迟补偿项和拓扑鲁棒项的复合 Lyapunov 函数，用于推导允许的最大延迟、最小驻留时间或平均切换频率条件。\n\n控制思路上，可设计一种分布式预测-阻尼型编队控制器。每个机器人仅使用邻居的延迟状态信息，并通过本地模型预测邻居当前状态；随后将预测误差、相对位姿误差和速度阻尼项共同注入控制律。为应对拓扑切换，可让机器人维护一个短时邻居记忆窗口：当通信边临时断开时，不立即丢弃该邻居约束，而是以随时间衰减的权重继续参与控制，从而减少拓扑突变带来的控制不连续。\n\n预期理论贡献是给出该分布式控制律在有界时变延迟和联合连通切换拓扑下的充分稳定条件，并分析延迟上界、拓扑切换速率、预测误差界与编队收敛速度之间的关系。实验上，可在仿真和多移动机器人平台中比较三类方法：无延迟补偿的一致性编队控制、仅预测补偿控制，以及所提出的预测-阻尼-邻居记忆控制，以验证其在高延迟和频繁掉链场景下对队形误差、控制平滑性和稳定裕度的改善。"} 
```
[result]: 已形成一个短篇研究提案：以延迟预测、阻尼注入和邻居记忆机制为核心，面向切换拓扑下的分布式编队稳定控制。

```action
    "control_strategy": "After detecting a generator trip, compute the estimated inertia deficit and dispatch adaptive virtual inertia and damping gains to renewable inverters, constrained by DC-link energy, current headroom, PLL stability margins, and rate-of-change-of-frequency limits.",
```

full_prompt: Propose a low-compute pipeline for metagenomic taxonomic profiling using Kraken2 and MetaPhlAn as baselines. Existing k-mer methods are fast but memory-heavy, while marker-gene methods can miss strain-level variation. Target comparable genus-level accuracy using less than half the RAM on standard laptop hardware.
chosen:
Rationale: I’ll do one targeted confirmation that compact/minimizer-based Kraken2 database strategies and marker-based profiling remain appropriate low-RAM baselines, then propose a hybrid laptop-oriented pipeline that preserves genus-level signal while avoiding full Kraken2 database RAM costs.

```action search
{"query":"2025 metagenomic taxonomic profiling Kraken2 low memory minimizer compact database MetaPhlAn marker gene genus accuracy"}
```

[Result] Recent Kraken2 documentation and metagenomic profiling discussions still emphasize that Kraken2 achieves speed via minimizers/compact hash tables but can require substantial RAM depending on database size; reduced/custom databases lower RAM at some sensitivity cost. MetaPhlAn remains a low-memory marker-gene profiler with strong species/genus profiling but reduced strain-resolution unless paired with strain tools. This supports a hybrid approach: host/filter + sketch-based triage + compact genus-focused Kraken2 database + MetaPhlAn reconciliation.

Rationale: The proposal should combine the speed of k-mer assignment with the low-memory specificity of marker genes, constrain the database to genus-level targets, and avoid strain-level overfitting while still retaining optional strain-variant evidence.
